boardQuestion: Which vertical should anchor the board narrative around governed AI and operator safety?
scorebookNarrative: AI governance is the clearest flagship lane for board-ready intelligence.
comparativeReality: The lane is strong, but still needs a tighter proof bundle to separate it from generic AI-advisory noise.
riskHeadline: AI governance is investable now, but the moat depends on keeping operator proof fresher than the market.
scoreSignal: Strong demand, strong proof, and strong executive attention are all aligned here.
missingProof: One tighter board memo and cross-sector proof bundle.
headline: AI governance is the strongest benchmark lane right now.
nextMove: Use AI governance as the benchmark reference lane for the rest of the portfolio.

boardQuestion: Which lane has the strongest reusable proof room and deal-readiness posture?
scorebookNarrative: Procurement is the best proof-reuse lane for buyer-facing scale.
comparativeReality: It is highly benchmarkable, though less category-defining than AI governance.
riskHeadline: Procurement is one of the safest lanes to scale because evidence reuse is already strong.
scoreSignal: Buyers, trust reviewers, and commercial teams can all consume the same outputs.
missingProof: One tighter benchmark story connecting trust, diligence, and commercial proof reuse.
headline: Procurement is the cleanest reusable trust and diligence lane.
nextMove: Package procurement as the proof-reuse benchmark for adjacent commercial surfaces.

boardQuestion: Which regulated lane is strongest enough to defend as a serious vertical, not just a curiosity?
scorebookNarrative: Biotech is a credible regulated-intelligence lane with strong seriousness signal.
comparativeReality: Proof is strong, but commercial pull and repeatability are still less visible than the flagship lanes.
riskHeadline: Biotech is credible, but should not be overstated as the primary expansion lane yet.
scoreSignal: The vertical signals seriousness and regulated-infrastructure depth to investors and buyers.
missingProof: A tighter benchmark story linking assay, CAPA, specimen, GxP, and safety into one expansion packet.
headline: Biotech is credible and useful, but not yet the top scaling lane.
nextMove: Package the regulated proof stack into one clearer benchmark brief.

boardQuestion: Where does the commercial story look good, but the benchmark proof is still thin?
scorebookNarrative: Payments, treasury, and merchant review create a credible commercial wedge.
comparativeReality: The lane has strong market language but still needs denser operator proof and lower execution drag.
riskHeadline: FinTech is attractive, but it is easier to oversell than to benchmark cleanly right now.
scoreSignal: Commercial pull is there, but the board story still outruns the proof depth slightly.
missingProof: A tighter cross-lane packet connecting payments, treasury, KYC, and merchant review.
headline: FinTech is promising, but not yet benchmark-tight.
nextMove: Tighten the FinTech comparison packet before treating it as a top-tier expansion lane.

boardQuestion: Which niche lane is more reusable than its surface area first suggests?
scorebookNarrative: Nonprofit is a reusable operating-shape lane, not just a one-off niche.
comparativeReality: The lane is repeatable, though the commercial ceiling is lower than AI governance or procurement.
riskHeadline: Nonprofit is strategically useful, but should stay framed as a pattern-strength lane, not the top growth engine.
scoreSignal: The lane proves the operating system can travel across sectors and still stay legible.
missingProof: A better benchmark packet showing how nonprofit patterns map to regulated and commercial lanes.
opportunityMoves: pair nonprofit with board narrative examples
headline: Nonprofit is more reusable than it first appears.
nextMove: Use nonprofit to prove operating-system transferability across very different sectors.

boardQuestion: Which lane is interesting but not yet strong enough to pull capital ahead of the better benchmarked clusters?
scorebookNarrative: Robotics proves ambition and operator seriousness.
comparativeReality: The lane is still too light to outrank procurement, AI governance, or regulated systems for near-term board focus.
riskHeadline: Robotics is a useful signal lane, but not a top investment priority yet.
scoreSignal: It helps with range and imagination, but the proof set is not yet dense enough to be a flagship.
missingProof: More repeatable deployment proof and a stronger board-ready value story.
opportunityMoves: avoid over-weighting it in the board story
headline: Robotics should stay in the portfolio, but not near the front of the board narrative.
nextMove: Keep building proof, but deprioritize it relative to stronger commercial and diligence lanes.
